task_completion.description: Agent opened the ticket details file to read the requirements.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor, file manager, or terminal window displaying the contents of ~/Desktop/ticket_details.txt.

task_completion.description: Agent logged into the OpenMRS system.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenMRS home screen or dashboard is visible in a web browser, showing the user is logged in.

task_completion.description: Agent navigated to the Encounter Types management page.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Manage Encounter Types' or similar metadata administration page in OpenMRS is visible in the browser.

task_completion.description: Agent entered the correct name and description for the new Encounter Type.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An 'Add Encounter Type' form is filled with the Name 'Telehealth Intake' and Description 'Initial patient assessment conducted via remote video connection.'.

task_completion.description: Agent saved the new Encounter Type and it is active.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Encounter Types list displays 'Telehealth Intake' and it appears as an active entry (e.g., not marked as retired or struck through).

integrity.description: Agent used a web browser to interact with OpenMRS.
integrity.visual_evidence: OpenMRS interface is shown inside a standard web browser (like Firefox or Chrome), not via terminal commands like curl.

integrity.description: Agent genuinely read the ticket file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent opening or catting the ticket_details.txt file, rather than just knowing the values.

integrity.description: Agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the final state.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser's developer tools (Inspect Element) are not open to manipulate the DOM to show the encounter type.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected encounter type Name is 'Telehealth Intake' and the Description is 'Initial patient assessment conducted via remote video connection.'
